identity.name: Chan Meng
identity.tagline: Building at the intersection of AI, cultural technology, and women's health.
identity.philosophy: Subtraction for life, addition for thought.
identity.voiceSummary: Architecture-grade vocabulary, evidence-first claims, restraint in life and boldness in expression. Cross-cultural fluency without translation.
identity.signaturePhrase: Subtraction for life, addition for thought.
audiences: Founders evaluating an AI agent architect / fractional CTO
Recruiters and hiring managers in AI / full-stack / FemTech
Engineers and learners following the open-source craft
Te Ao Māori partners and cultural-technology collaborators

display.role: Display, headings, and stat numbers — heavy MIXED-CASE compact grotesque, tight. The magazine-cover voice.
display.licenseNote: Anton is OFL (Google Fonts) and the closest free match to the commercial PP Neue Corp Compact Ultrabold — heavy, condensed, mixed-case. Oswald 700 / Bebas Neue are deeper fallbacks. Single weight (400) renders intrinsically ultra-bold; never faux-bold.

bodySans.role: Body, navigation, labels — DM Sans 500, the workhorse.
bodySans.licenseNote: DM Sans & Inter are OFL. Segoe UI is the Windows fallback.

mono.role: Data signatures only — datestamps, paths, version numbers. House extension (Caldera has no mono).
mono.licenseNote: JetBrains Mono is OFL. Consolas is the Windows fallback.

displayXl.note: Magazine-cover hero headline (web only). Anton, full-width, mixed-case.

displayLg.note: Large section / sub-hero headline. Anton, mixed-case.

display.note: Hero / page name. Anton compact ultrabold, mixed-case.

h3.note: Entry / card titles. DM Sans medium.

body.note: Default body. Never below this size. DM Sans 500.

eyebrow.note: Small uppercased label. DM Sans bold.

principles: Display is heavy compact grotesque (Anton) — the name, every heading, and stat numbers; MIXED case (not all-caps), 0.02em tracking on display only.
Orange is a BLOCK background or accent rule, never small body or link text — links and labels are ink.
Violet is decorative-only — large background shapes and cards, never text or UI.
Flat by default: hierarchy comes from solid color blocks, not shadows or gradients.
Body never sits below 16px on web or 9pt in print; DM Sans 500 is the workhorse.
Mono is reserved for data signatures — datestamps, paths, version numbers.

monogram.note: Used as section divider mark — see signatures.monogramDivider.

doNot: Do not recolor the logo — black ink on basalt / ash is canonical.
Do not place the logo on photographic backgrounds without an ash mat.
Do not skew, rotate, or apply effects (shadows, glows, gradients).
Do not pair the logo with a generated wordmark — the SVG IS the wordmark.

principles.name: Architecture-grade vocabulary
principles.rule: Prefer engineering nouns (orchestration, dispatch, hub-and-spoke) over marketing adjectives (powerful, robust, world-class).
principles.why: Chan's existing CV uses precise nouns; recruiters who matter recognise them.

principles.name: Evidence-first
principles.rule: Every claim links to one proof point (PR, repo, live URL, paper). Adjectives without links get cut.
principles.why: Established by the README redesign spec; carry forward.

principles.name: Subtraction for life, addition for thought
principles.rule: At most one orange accent per visual unit; remove decoration until what is left is the idea.
principles.why: Chan's stated personal philosophy; the brand must enact it even while the surface is bold.

principles.name: Cross-cultural fluency without translation
principles.rule: Te reo Māori, 中文, English appear in their own forms — italicised once, then treated as first-class vocabulary. No parenthetical glosses unless the audience demonstrably needs them.
principles.why: Existing CV does this with 'Te Whare Tapa Whā'; codify.

principles.name: Pace by white space
principles.rule: Visual rhythm beats density. The 61.8/38.2 ratio in the CV is canonical; default to it elsewhere too.
principles.why: Existing theme.typ already chose this; promote it from CV-only to system-wide.

principles.name: Never apologise for restraint
principles.rule: No 'sorry it's plain', no 'minimal-but-' qualifiers. The confidence is the statement.
principles.why: Restraint loses its meaning the moment it is hedged.

do: Recruited by Anthropic for the Claude residency programme.
86.6% solo-built; AI mentor matching and membership-flow automation in production.
Replaces click-through workflows with agentic ones: natural-language intent drives downstream orchestration.
dont: Leveraged cutting-edge AI to deliver robust, world-class solutions.
I'm a passionate, results-driven full-stack rockstar.

heroBand.doNotUseWhen: Internal sections. Multiple per page. Cards.

goldEyebrow.rule: eyebrow scale + ink text + a short orange pixelArt rule above a display heading
goldEyebrow.useWhen: Above any section that opens with display type. Above the hero subhead.
goldEyebrow.doNotUseWhen: Inline body. More than one orange mark per visual unit.

monoDatestamp.rule: mono scale 13px in ink; format: 'last updated · YYYY-MM-DD · build N'
monoDatestamp.useWhen: Footer of every long-form surface (README, CV, brand-system, future webpages).

monogramDivider.useWhen: Between major narrative shifts (e.g. between 'Featured work' and 'Open source').
monogramDivider.doNotUseWhen: Between sibling cards or list items.

halftone.useWhen: Hero decorative panel; blog / project card thumbnails.
halftone.doNotUseWhen: Behind body text. As a button fill. More than one hero panel per surface.

dotGrid.useWhen: Section / page backgrounds behind cards — the printed-paper ground.
dotGrid.doNotUseWhen: Directly behind body text at full strength; inside cards.

dottedEdge.useWhen: Secondary buttons, collapsed accordion rows, vertical dividers, carousel arrows.
dottedEdge.doNotUseWhen: Primary actions (those are a solid orange fill, never a dotted outline).

connectedCards.connectorShape: dog-bone SVG path (smooth radius-8 concave fillets, no centre rect — a square sliver protrudes); 16px wide in the gap, ~47% of card height; rotates 90° when cards stack
connectedCards.responsive: bones PERSIST at every breakpoint (verified live) — row + vertical bones → 2×2 CENTRE CROSS-JUNCTION (vertical bones link the top+bottom pairs, horizontal bones link the left+right pairs) → single column + a horizontal bone between every stacked pair
connectedCards.useWhen: A tight row of closely-related metric / stat cards.
connectedCards.doNotUseWhen: Unrelated cards; single cards; content cards with long copy. Never fill these cards orange — orange lives only in the icon badge.

interaction.transition: duration.base (200ms) · easing.standard for color/border; duration.fast (120ms) for transform

hover.ghost: invert to a solid ink fill, label flips to canvas
hover.dotted: border solidifies to orange + orange label
hover.carouselArrow: button fills orange, the ink arrow inverts to white

active.card: selected/active tile flips to a solid Digital Orange fill with white text

focus.rule: keyboard :focus-visible shows the orange focus ring (shadow.focusRing); pill inputs thicken the border to 2px — no glow on mouse interaction

interaction.reducedMotion: honour prefers-reduced-motion: reduce — disable all transitions/animation

pillRotation.doNotUseWhen: Inside body prose. As decoration. Outside Chan's own tools.

freeTools.text: Free tools I built for the community

imagery.direction: Functional and abstract, with a risograph print feel. The signature treatment is a HALFTONE DOT GRADIENT — a two-stop ramp from Digital Orange (#FC5000) to Cyber Violet (#524AE9) rendered as a field of dots whose radius scales across the gradient, clipped to a 40px-rounded panel. Used for hero panels and card thumbnails (see public/brand/halftone-*.svg). Product screenshots and logos render as monochrome ink outlines or white fills inside ash-white cards — let the UI be protagonist. Flat and printed, never glossy; depth comes from surface color shifts, not shadows. Real assets over stock.
approved: /public/tech-logos/ (third-party tech stack marks — used in pill-row context only)
forbidden: Trophy / shields.io / wakatime / GitHub-streak badges (external decoration services)

readme.canvas: GitHub renders white; the basalt canvas applies only to images we control
readme.fontHint: GitHub controls typography — semantic tokens here are advisory for img/SVG only

socialCard.note: Square / 1.91:1 export. Bebas name + DM Sans label, one orange rule/chip.
